# Demonstration: Static Analysis vs Dynamic Execution Gap

# This scripttest executes several programs
exec echo "Hello from scripttest"
stdout 'Hello'

exec mcpdiff --help
stderr 'Usage'

# Create a Go program that analyzes itself
mkdir -p demo

# Create analyzer.go using multiple echo commands
exec echo 'package main' > demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo 'import (' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    "fmt"' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    "go/ast"' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    "go/parser"' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    "go/token"' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo ')' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo 'func main() {' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    fmt.Println("Static analysis sees function calls...")' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    src := `package main' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo 'import "os/exec"' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo 'func runTest() {' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    cmd := exec.Command("mcpdiff", "--help")' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    cmd.Run()' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '}' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '`' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    ' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    // Parse the source' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    fset := token.NewFileSet()' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    node, _ := parser.ParseFile(fset, "", src, 0)' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    ' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    // Find exec.Command calls' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    ast.Inspect(node, func(n ast.Node) bool {' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '        if call, ok := n.(*ast.CallExpr); ok {' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '            if sel, ok := call.Fun.(*ast.SelectorExpr); ok {' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '                if sel.Sel.Name == "Command" {' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '                    fmt.Println("Found exec.Command call")' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '                    // But we cannot determine at compile time what it will execute!' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '                    fmt.Println("Cannot determine target binary statically")' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '                }' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '            }' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '        }' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '        return true' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '    })' >> demo/analyzer.go
exec echo '}' >> demo/analyzer.go

exec go run demo/analyzer.go
stdout 'Found exec.Command'
stdout 'Cannot determine target binary statically'

# This demonstrates the fundamental limitation:
# 1. This scripttest executes echo and mcpdiff
# 2. Static analysis of this test would only see "exec" commands
# 3. It can't determine which specific binaries are executed
# 4. Traditional callgraph tools miss these dynamic connections
